Title: Methylation of MdMYB1 locus mediated by RdDM pathway regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Orin callus; red-flesh apple callus; drm2 mutant background
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: domains rearranged methyltransferase 2; de novo DNA methyltransferase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit skin/flesh coloration and antioxidant/nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-IP; pull-down; overexpression in apple callus; heterologous complementation in Arabidopsis drm2 mutant; methylation assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes CHH methylation of MdMYB1/MdMYB10 promoters, repressing anthocyanin pathway gene expression and reducing anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdDRM2-1 interacts with MdAGO4-1/2 and MdRDM1 in the RdDM complex. 2. Overexpression in apple callus increased CHH methylation of the MdMYB1 promoter and reduced expression of MdMYB1 and structural anthocyanin genes. 3. Expression in Arabidopsis drm2 rescued CHH methylation defects at AGO4-binding loci.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: acts as the de novo methyltransferase catalyzing CHH methylation in the RdDM pathway
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: lower in sports than in parents during apple coloration
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: increased expression reduces anthocyanin accumulation and yields paler calli
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: bag removal/light context for fruit coloration; transgenic overexpression in callus
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target for epigenetic modulation of fruit coloration; lowering activity may enhance anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: classified as common gene because it is an epigenetic methyltransferase rather than a pathway enzyme or transcription factor.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Orin callus; red-flesh apple callus; drm2 mutant background
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: domains rearranged methyltransferase 2; de novo DNA methyltransferase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit skin/flesh coloration and antioxidant/nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-IP; pull-down; overexpression in apple callus; heterologous complementation in Arabidopsis drm2 mutant; methylation assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes CHH methylation of MdMYB1/MdMYB10 promoters, repressing anthocyanin pathway gene expression and reducing anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdDRM2-2 interacts with MdAGO4-1/2 and MdRDM1 in the RdDM complex. 2. Overexpression in apple callus increased CHH methylation of the MdMYB1 promoter and reduced expression of MdMYB1 and structural anthocyanin genes. 3. Expression in Arabidopsis drm2 rescued CHH methylation defects at AGO4-binding loci.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: acts as the de novo methyltransferase catalyzing CHH methylation in the RdDM pathway
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: lower in sports than in parents during apple coloration
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: increased expression reduces anthocyanin accumulation and yields paler calli
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: bag removal/light context for fruit coloration; transgenic overexpression in callus
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target for epigenetic modulation of fruit coloration; lowering activity may enhance anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: classified as common gene because it is an epigenetic methyltransferase rather than a pathway enzyme or transcription factor.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit skin/flesh coloration and antioxidant/nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-IP; pull-down; expression analysis in transgenic calli
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports RdDM complex formation associated with CHH methylation of MdMYB1 promoter and repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdRDM1 interacts with MdAGO4-1/2 and MdDRM2-1/2 in vivo and in vitro. 2. The study supports MdRDM1 as a component of the apple RdDM effector complex linked to MdMYB1 promoter methylation. 3. Transcript levels were elevated in transgenic calli overexpressing MdAGO4s/MdDRM2s.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: component of the RdDM effector complex mediating CHH methylation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: no particular pattern reported during apple coloration
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible auxiliary target for modifying epigenetic control of anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: functional evidence in this paper is for protein interaction and complex membership rather than independent perturbation of anthocyanin phenotype.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: largest subunit of RNA polymerase V
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for MdAGO4 binding to the methylated MR3 region, indicating lncRNA-mediated targeting in RdDM; not required for direct binding to the c3 ATATCAGA site
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdNRPE1 knockdown inhibited MdAGO4 binding to the MR3 region of the MdMYB1 promoter. 2. MdNRPE1 knockdown did not affect MdAGO4 binding to the c3 region containing the direct DNA-binding motif. 3. These results support coexistence of Pol V/lncRNA-guided and direct DNA-binding modes of MdAGO4 targeting.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: mediates Pol V-dependent lncRNA production required for one mode of RdDM targeting
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: knocked down by RNAi in transgenic calli
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for AGO4 transgenes; RNAi construct for MdNRPE1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for manipulating epigenetic repression of anthocyanin regulators, though likely with broad genome-wide effects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: included as a core common gene because functional perturbation directly informed the mechanism of MdMYB1 methylation targeting in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit skin coloration and antioxidant/nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdCHS; MdCHI; MdF3H; MdDFR; MdANS; MdUFGT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates anthocyanin structural genes; lower MdMYB1 expression is associated with lower expression of MdCHS, MdCHI, MdF3H, MdDFR, MdANS and MdUFGT
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression profiling after bag removal; promoter methylation analysis by McrBC-PCR and BSP; correlation analysis with anthocyanin content
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher MdMYB1 expression promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red fruit skin coloration; promoter CHH methylation suppresses MdMYB1 expression and reduces anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB1 transcript abundance was highest in the most highly pigmented cultivar and closely paralleled anthocyanin accumulation. 2. CHH methylation in the MR3 region of the MdMYB1 promoter was negatively correlated with MdMYB1 expression and anthocyanin content. 3. RdDM components increased CHH methylation at the MdMYB1 promoter and reduced MdMYB1 expression, linking epigenetic silencing of MdMYB1 to reduced anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CHH methylation of the MR3 promoter region (-1246 to -780) negatively regulates MdMYB1 transcription; methylation is mediated by the RdDM pathway involving MdAGO4s, MdDRM2s, MdRDM1 and MdNRPE1-dependent lncRNA guidance for MR3 binding
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdbHLH3; MdbHLH33; MdAGO4-1; MdAGO4-2; MdDRM2-1; MdDRM2-2; MdRDM1; MdNRPE1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly expressed among tested regulatory genes in fruit skin after bag removal; peaks at 12 days after bag removal; highest in Yanfu 8
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fruit skin after bag removal; strongly induced during coloration; reduced in MdAGO4/MdDRM2 overexpression calli
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: bag removal/light exposure during fruit coloration
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis differences among the three apple varieties
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target for improving apple coloration and anthocyanin content via epigenome editing or selection for low promoter methylation haplotypes; should consider RdDM-mediated silencing effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study identifies MdMYB1 as the downstream anthocyanin regulator whose promoter methylation, rather than coding sequence variation, explains coloration differences among apple sports.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Orin callus; red-flesh apple callus; ago4 mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: RNA-directed DNA methylation effector / Argonaute protein
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Argonaute 4 family; Argonaute N domain; PAZ domain; Piwi domain
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms RdDM effector complex with MdDRM2-1/MdDRM2-2 and MdRDM1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit skin and flesh coloration; antioxidant/nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: CHH DNA methylation-mediated repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis regulator
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: lncRNAs produced by Pol V/MdNRPE1 for binding to MR3 region; can also directly bind promoter DNA sequence
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: RdDM-mediated transcriptional repression via promoter binding and recruitment of DNA methylation machinery
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes CHH methylation of the MdMYB1 promoter, lowering MdMYB1 and downstream anthocyanin structural gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; overexpression in apple callus; heterologous complementation in Arabidopsis ago4 mutant; methylation assays; RNAi of MdNRPE1; siRNA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increases CHH methylation of the MdMYB1 promoter and suppresses anthocyanin pathway gene expression, causing paler apple calli and reduced anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdAGO4-1 binds the MdMYB1 promoter in vitro and in vivo, with enrichment at the c3 region and binding to MR3. 2. MdAGO4-1 interacts with MdDRM2-1/2 and MdRDM1 to form an RdDM effector complex. 3. Overexpression in apple callus increased CHH methylation of the MdMYB1 promoter and reduced anthocyanin pathway gene expression; expression in Arabidopsis ago4 rescued CHH methylation at AGO4-binding loci. 4. EMSA showed direct recognition of the ATATCAGA motif, and the Piwi domain was required for DNA binding.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: no particular pattern reported during apple coloration
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: represses anthocyanin accumulation and produces paler red callus; possible undesirable reduction in fruit coloration if elevated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: bag removal/light context for fruit coloration; transgenic overexpression in callus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key RdDM factor that directly targets the MdMYB1 locus to impose CHH methylation and repress anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for manipulating apple skin/flesh coloration through epigenetic engineering; downregulation could relieve MdMYB1 silencing and enhance anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binding to MR3 was reduced by MdNRPE1 knockdown, but binding to the c3 ATATCAGA-containing region persisted, supporting both lncRNA-guided and direct DNA binding modes.
